recommendation.summary: c1v is an AI-native PRD + system-design tool. The synthesizer recommends a Vercel-deployed Next.js front-end orchestrated by LangGraph, using Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.5 as the spec-generation LLM and pgvector (Supabase Postgres) for retrieval. This pairing was selected from the M4 Pareto frontier (AV.01 dominates AV.02..AV.05 across cost/latency/availability) because it satisfies the 3-second p95 user-facing budget (interface_specs chain AUTHORING_SPEC_EMIT sums 2600 ms) while keeping the 100% citation-floor (NFR.01) achievable through grounded-retrieval gating. The recommendation carries 14 high-RPN residual risks from M8.b that ride alongside the architecture; each is mapped to a landed control surfaced via M5 form_function_map.
recommendation.rationale:
D-01: Sonnet 4.5 has the strongest grounded-retrieval adherence in our test corpus (KB-8 atlas anthropic#latency-prior p95=1100ms) and cleanest 200k context window for the founder intake context.

D-02: Co-locating vectors with relational state in a single Supabase Postgres collapses two network hops into one. KB-8 atlas supabase#availability-prior 99.99 satisfies NFR.04.

D-03: LangGraph allows DN.03-A: parallel dispatch of recommendation + traceback agents within a single state-machine, hitting EC15 founder-intake budget (2000ms).

D-04: Vercel removes ops surface entirely (PC.1) and provides Next.js-native streaming for EC16 chunk cadence (50ms).

risks.description: [MEDIUM] Spec generation hallucinates citations unsupported by DOCUMENTATION SOURCES — Detectability remains imperfect — silent hallucinations within retrieved corpus still possible until SR-CORNELL validator agent ships.
[MEDIUM LOW] Approval fires without compliance policy check —

[MEDIUM] Adapter exceeds non-invasive overhead budget on customer system — Sampled-vs-full-fidelity ingest tradeoff (fmea_early.FM.04.M2) NOT yet committed — customer overhead under burst load still possible.
[MEDIUM HIGH] Metric stream gap undetected (silent loss) —
[MEDIUM] Recommendation cadence exceeds RECOMMENDATION_CADENCE_MIN (60 min, est.) — RECOMMENDATION_CADENCE_MIN not yet promoted to NFR (FM.06.M2 deferred) — no contract-level enforcement.
[MEDIUM] Traceback emits recommendation without complete citation chain —
[MEDIUM LOW] CLI bundle (DE.06) ships with secrets embedded —
[MEDIUM] Recommendation loop (DE.10 → F.2) creates specification drift —
[MEDIUM LOW] LLM provider outage (DE.13 unavailable) halts spec generation —

[MEDIUM] Primary/fallback LLM split-brain: divergent specs on transient primary degradation — No request-level provider stickiness; circuit-breaker (interface_specs IF.01..IF.10 error_rate_threshold_pct=25) trips at session granularity but does not pin a single founder session to one provider. Recommended next-cycle ADR: pin (founder_id, project_id) → provider for session lifetime.

[MEDIUM] Event-bus cascade failure: IF.06 streaming backlog starves IF.10 feedback loop — No isolation between IF.06 and IF.10 runtimes in chosen LangGraph topology. ADR candidate: separate consumer pool for streaming vs in-process interfaces, or migrate IF.06 to dedicated worker process.

[MEDIUM HIGH] pgvector index drift: HNSW recall degrades silently after corpus growth — No periodic recall benchmark scheduled. ADR candidate: add weekly recall@k probe over canonical query set; trigger HNSW rebuild when recall drops below 0.9.

[MEDIUM] Vercel cold-start tax violates IF.06 streaming SLA on FR.06 adapter — Vercel free/pro tier cold-start latency is non-contractual. ADR candidate: warm FR.06 via scheduled ping or migrate FR.06 to long-running Cloud Run (matches identity-api pattern in monorepo recommendation).

tradeoffs.accepted: Claude Sonnet 4.5 + pgvector + LangGraph + Vercel: cost 320 USD/month at 100 DAU | latency 2600 ms p95 end-to-end | avail 99.9 % uptime
GPT-4 Turbo + Pinecone + LangChain Chains + Cloud Run: cost 378 USD/month at 100 DAU | latency 2810 ms p95 end-to-end | avail 99.9 % uptime
OpenRouter federated + Weaviate self-hosted + Custom orch + Fly.io: cost 240 USD/month at 100 DAU | latency 3100 ms p95 end-to-end | avail 99.5 % uptime
